summary: Iteration 87 — verified the 5 NEW items from this wave (A1 fee-schedule price_sources, A3 server-side provider search, B1 quick-submit endpoint, B2 ClaimDetail button, B3 ScribePanel button). Backend: 10/10 tests passing (test_quick_submit.py 3/3 + new test_iter87_wave_b.py 7/7). Frontend: live verification of A3 (server-side ?q= debounced search) and B2 (button rendered with correct disabled gating). B3 verified by code review + transitive backend coverage. No defects found; sandbox CHC adapter returns synthetic chc-sbx-{hex} external_ids as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: ClaimDetail.jsx claim-quick-submit-btn correctly gates on `['draft','validation_failed','ready'].includes(claim.status)` — verified disabled on a 'submitted' claim (1f6a68bb).

AITemplatesPage.jsx now does server-side debounced search: live test typing 'noah' fired exactly one GET /api/auth/users?role=doctor&limit=200&q=noah and returned Dr. Noah Carter. Loading state ('Searching…') and empty state ('No matches') are rendered inside SelectContent — only visible while the dropdown is open, which is acceptable UX.

ScribePanel.jsx: scribe-quick-submit-btn wired to quickSubmitClaim helper from useClaims.js; renders alongside 'Open claim →' link after send-to-claim success. Backend chain (send-to-claim → quick-submit) covered end-to-end by test_quick_submit.py::test_happy_path_via_scribe_send_to_claim (passing).

Send-to-claim now returns price_sources array; entries are plain strings ('catalog' / 'fee_schedule' / 'zero') rather than dicts — fine, matches doctor's intent of seeing where each line price came from. len(price_sources) == lines is enforced in test.

Quick-submit endpoint correctly returns 409 on already-submitted claim (verified via second call), 403/404 on staff role, 404 on unknown claim. Sandbox CHC returns adapter_external_id matching `chc-sbx-...` pattern.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on A3 + B2 live; B3 via code review + transitive backend

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1, doctor@ccms.app / Doctor@ComplianceClinic1, staff@ccms.app / Staff@ComplianceClinic1
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Riverbend follow-up notes and CHC-routed payers. test_iter87_wave_b reuses the same seed lookup helpers as test_quick_submit.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 new wave items verified. Mocked: Change Healthcare adapter is in sandbox mode (CLEARINGHOUSE_CHC_MODE=sandbox in /app/backend/.env) and emits synthetic 'chc-sbx-{12hex}' external_ids without a real network call — INTENTIONAL per user choice B3c. Backend tests assert this prefix when adapter_route=='change_healthcare'. test_iter87_wave_b.py is reusable for future regression. The TestQuickSubmitEdgeCases class consumes a draft note per run (transitions it to submitted via successful quick-submit) — make sure new draft notes are seedable if running back-to-back. No regressions detected on iteration_86's three flows (NL reschedule/cancel, AITemplates, Scribe send-to-claim).
